text: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
Bananas are slightly radioactive due to their high potassium content.
The human body contains enough carbon to make about 9,000 pencils.
Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and nine brains (one central and one in each arm).
Water can boil and freeze at the same time — this phenomenon is called the 'triple point'.
The speed of light is about 299,792,458 metres per second — fast enough to circle Earth 7.5 times in one second.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
Crows are so intelligent they can recognise human faces and hold grudges against people who treat them poorly.
There are more atoms in a grain of sand than there are grains of sand on all the world's beaches.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
The fax machine was invented in 1843 — before the telephone.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire. Teaching at Oxford began in 1096; the Aztec Empire was founded in 1428.
Napoleon was once attacked by a horde of rabbits. He had ordered a rabbit hunt and his staff collected hundreds of tame bunnies by mistake.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye — that's a myth debunked by astronauts.
Ancient Romans used crushed mouse brains as toothpaste.
Viking warriors wore no horned helmets — that image was popularised by a 19th-century costume designer.
The first computer bug was a real bug — a moth found trapped in a relay of the Harvard Mark II computer in 1947.
The shortest war in history lasted just 38–45 minutes: the Anglo-Zanzibar War of 1896.
Ancient Egyptians used slabs of stone as pillows.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart. This is called 'rafting'.
A snail can sleep for up to three years when conditions are too dry or cold.
Elephants are the only animals that can't jump.
Wombats produce cube-shaped poop — the only animal known to do so.
A group of cats is called a clowder; a group of kittens is called a kindle.
Cows have best friends and become stressed when separated from them.
Polar bear fur is not white — it's actually transparent and hollow. It appears white because it scatters light.
The mantis shrimp can throw a punch at 23 m/s — fast enough to boil the water around it with cavitation bubbles.
Butterflies taste with their feet, using sensors called chemoreceptors.
Sharks are older than trees. Sharks have existed for about 450 million years; trees appeared around 350 million years ago.
One million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches.
The footprints left by Apollo astronauts on the Moon will last for millions of years — there's no wind or rain to erode them.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus — it rotates so slowly that it completes an orbit before one full rotation.
Neutron stars are so dense that a teaspoon of their material would weigh about 10 million tonnes on Earth.
The Olympus Mons volcano on Mars is three times the height of Mount Everest.
Space is completely silent because there is no medium for sound waves to travel through.
Saturn's rings are not solid — they are made of billions of ice and rock fragments ranging in size from a grain of sand to a house.
The International Space Station travels at 28,000 km/h, completing 16 sunrises and sunsets every day.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
The first computer mouse was made of wood and had a single button. It was invented by Doug Engelbart in 1964.
Email is older than the World Wide Web. Ray Tomlinson sent the first email in 1971; Tim Berners-Lee invented the web in 1989.
The first domain name ever registered was Symbolics.com on 15 March 1985.
Nintendo was founded in 1889 — they originally made playing cards.
The average smartphone today has more computing power than NASA used to put humans on the Moon in 1969.
QWERTY keyboard layout was designed in the 1870s to slow typists down and prevent typewriter jams.
The first YouTube video, 'Me at the zoo', was uploaded on 23 April 2005 and is just 18 seconds long.
Google processes around 8.5 billion search queries per day — roughly 99,000 per second.
The Python programming language is named after Monty Python's Flying Circus, not the snake.
Wi-Fi doesn't stand for 'Wireless Fidelity' — it's simply a brand name coined by a marketing agency and has no formal meaning.
A group of owls is called a parliament.
In 1386, a pig was executed by public hanging in France for the murder of a child.
The tongue is the fastest-healing part of the human body.
It's impossible to hum while holding your nose.
Bubble wrap was originally invented as wallpaper in 1957.
A 'jiffy' is an actual unit of time: 1/100th of a second.
The plastic tips on shoelaces are called aglets. Their primary purpose is to make laces easier to thread.
Cereal boxes are 30% air by volume on average — manufacturers call this 'slack fill'.
The inventor of the Pringles can is buried in one. Fredric Baur's ashes were interred in a Pringles tube in 2008.
In Switzerland, it's illegal to own just one guinea pig — they are social animals and must have a companion.
